posts.author: Dynam (lus)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am looking to integrate Microsoft Dynamics Navision with monday to have data move from monday.com to Microsoft Dynamics Navision and vice versa. It could be directly from Microsoft Dynamics Navision or Microsoft SQL.

Either via make or zapier.

posts.author: CarbonJon (Jon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Dynam !

The team here at CarbonWeb would love to assist you with this integration. We are experts in making custom API connections with make.com! posts.author: patrick7 (Patrick Kool)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Dynam,

I’m happy to help! I have more than 4 years of experience with both Make.com & Monday.com and can code in Python or Javascript in case either of them are limited in what we need it to do.

I recently finished a two way synchronisation project between Monday.com and Cin7

Feel free to schedule a call here so we can discuss your request!

Greetings,

Patrick
You can learn more about me on LinkedIn.

P.S.
I wouldn’t recommend Zapier in this case, because two way synchronisation can take a lot of tasks/zaps and the monthly costs of Zapier can become very high. I also find it’s not flexible enough for these synchronisation automations.

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
To integrate Microsoft Dynamics Navision with Monday.com for seamless data flow between the two systems, we’ll leverage Make.com or Zapier. We’ll establish two-way synchronization, allowing data to move effortlessly from Monday.com to Microsoft Dynamics Navision and vice versa, ensuring both platforms are consistently updated. This can be achieved through direct integrations using Microsoft SQL for robust data handling or using API connectors to bridge the two platforms efficiently.

We can set up automation workflows that trigger data transfer events based on specific conditions, like updates or new entries in either system. This will involve configuring Make.com or Zapier to handle data mapping, error handling, and scheduling to maintain data integrity and reliability. Key steps include:

  • Identifying critical data fields and mapping them accurately.
  • Setting up triggers and actions for real-time data syncing.
  • Implementing error handling mechanisms to manage any data discrepancies.

posts.author: ardyasadw (ardyasadw)
posts.cookedHtml: I can help set up a link between your boards and Navision by sending data through SQL. If you already have access to the database, syncing both ways is doable with custom work.

posts.author: ardyasadw (ardyasadw)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve hooked monday.com into Navision before by pushing data through SQL, and it worked fine once the mapping was clean. Make does the heavy lifting better than Zapier in this case, especially if you need bidirectional sync. I’ve also had a smoother time keeping everything stable by using some of the best add-ons for Business Central to keep data tidy before it even hits monday.com, which saved me a few headaches.
